reads: The content is Minister Cañedo's circular ordering the arrest of Lorenzo de Zavala, "Gob.n q.e era del estado de Mégico" — impossible before 1830 (the state was created 1824; Zavala's persecution under Bustamante began 1830).
note: The 1823 heading is off by seven years; cataloged under 1830.

was: Mathes's published edition prints the Moraga Bodega reconnaissance diary under 1812.
reads: The manuscript dateline reads 3 Oct 1810 (crop-verified) — in 1810 the vessel at Bodega was "frag.ta no… rusa sino Americana": an American otter camp, two years before Fort Ross.
note: A two-year misdate in the standard published edition, proven at the leaf.

reads: This letter survives only in Ivan Petroff's 19th-century English translation; Petroff's reliability is questioned in the literature.
note: Quote the Spanish copy of the same exchange (see the record's concordance) rather than Petroff's English where possible.

was: The 1786 otter tariff transcribed with prices ×100 too low (pesos read as cents), first-class pelts "≥1½ varas."
reads: Full-resolution re-read: 1st class (pelts of 1¼ vara) 10/9/8/7 pesos; 2nd class (una vara) …
note: The whole schedule re-transcribed from a full-res crop.

reads: The dateline abbreviation reads "Setbre" — September 1786.
note: One of three adjacent Goycoechea letters (348–350) with the same misread; each header checked individually — docs 341–344 are genuinely October.

reads: "Setbre" — September 1786.
"Setbre" — September 1786.
"Sbre" — 30 September 1815 (crop-verified).
March 1813 (crop-verified).
Dated 10 December 1809 from the leaf.
"Sbre" — September (matches Doc 351's glyph).
The manuscript resumen reads 37 (grand total 432 confirms it).
"desea ajustar con las Misiones 25,000 @ de sebos, cueros de res y otros productos" — 25,000 (numeral crop-matched).
"Sobre que se imponga leve castigo á indios" — leve, re-read at full resolution.
"353 hombres" (full-res crop; matches the arithmetic 323+~30 and the $92,593 figure).

was: Gap-read record: orig. pp. 122–24 (the otter/Catalina narrative, 1804).
reads: Leaf-verified 2026-07-12: every leaf of C-A 25 carries TWO numbering systems — the original tomo pages (which this record's 122–24 gives) and a handwritten per-tomo LEAF number top-right. Bancroft's footnotes cite the LEAF numbers: his 'Prov. Rec., MS., ix. 122-3' (Hist. Cal. II:90 n.21) is NOT this document but Arrillaga's 28 Jun 1810 Suisun-campaign report at tomo leaves 122–123 = scans n374–375 (now ca25-d37).
note: Applies to all 36 gap-read records of this volume: their orig_page values are original tomo pages, their scan links run ~2 behind the true BookReader position, and Tomo X records were mislabeled Tomo IX (fixed). Full re-verification queued.

reads: Added 2026-07-12 after the resolver audit: Bancroft's 'Prov. Rec., MS., ix. 122-3' resolved to the wrong documents because the catalog and Bancroft use different page systems in this volume. This record was located and read at the leaf (scans n374–375, handwritten leaf numbers 122–123, orig. pp. 367–71).
note: The tomo_leaf field carries Bancroft's cited numbering; the resolver indexes both systems for this volume.

was: Summary read Alférez Argüello's departure from the Santa Bárbara company as "going as *sereno* to the San Francisco presidio company."
reads: Full-resolution crop (2026-07-17, blind sample audit #10/200): "quien va de Ten.te á la compañía del Pres.º de S.n Fran.co" — he went as TENIENTE (a promotion; José Darío Argüello's attested 1787 transfer), not as sereno.
note: Found by the blind 200-record sample audit (sample committed sha256:70765ef5…). Class: abbreviated-rank misread (Ten.te).

reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #15/200, 2026-07-17): the dateline reads "1789. Mayo 18" — May 18, not 12.
note: Class: day-digit misread (8 read as 2). Sample committed sha256:70765ef5…

was: The dispute principals recorded as "Domingo Olvera" vs "Fr. José Lapuente."
reads: The leaf (n268) and the case header (n266, full-resolution crops, 2026-07-17) read "D. Domingo OTERO" and "el P. LAFUENTE" — Otero, not Olvera; Lafuente, not Lapuente.
note: Found by the blind sample audit (#16/200). Same correction applies to ca6-d190 (the dispute's opening record). Class: name misread.

was: "Domingo Olvera vs. Fr. José Lapuente" debt dispute.
reads: Case header (n266): "diferencias entre el P. Lafuente y Domingo Otero."
note: Collateral to the ca6-d191 sample-audit finding. Class: name misread.

reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #21/200, 2026-07-17): "un nuevo Mayordomo en aquella Misión llamado VELARDE" — twice on the leaf.
note: Class: name misread. Incident substance (harboring the sirviente José Barajas, the sable, the arrest) verified exact.

reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #24/200, 2026-07-17): "1797. Mayo 18. Orizaba" — May 18, not 13.
note: Class: terminal digit 8 misread (same class as ca5-d7's 18→12). Watch the 8s.

reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #25/200, 2026-07-17): "1795 = Abril 8 = Sta Bárbara" — April 8, not 6.
note: THIRD terminal-digit-8 misread found by the audit (18→12, 18→13, 8→6): Savage's open-top 8 is the catalog's one systematic glyph weakness. A targeted 8-sweep is queued.

reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #37/200, 2026-07-17): "1805 Marzo 7. Méx." — March, not May.
note: Class: Marzo/Mayo word confusion (new class).

was: "Juan Antonio Maya" arrived in the brig San Antonio, property of "Don José Carvencia[?]."
reads: The leaf (n172) reads MAYO three times (incl. the signed postscript on the preceding letter), and CAVENECIA twice.
note: Sample audit #39/200. Maya→Mayo = name misread; the flagged Carvencia[?] resolves to Cavenecia (the [?] did its job).

reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #41/200, 2026-07-17): "1819 = Durango 18 de Dcbre" — December; the letterform matches the same leaf's "Diciembre 2" entry, and the letter follows it as a Cosak-voyage update.
note: Class: 'bre-family month abbreviation (Dcbre read as October) — in the rating<=2 stratum the 2026-07-17 Setbre sweep's stated bounds did not cover.

was: Scan pin n218 for the Thomas Pearce / Broughton mention (orig. p. xxii.~482).
reads: n218 carries tomo pp. ~559–562 (San Diego fort works; Feb 1795 letters) — not this content. Page interpolation (n190 = p.428, n208 = p.531) places p.~482 near leaves n199–n200.
note: Blind sample audit #44/200 (2026-07-18): the audit's first broken scan pin (43 prior pins correct). Survey-sample row; content to be re-located and re-pinned at the C-A 14 residual pass.

reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #52/200, 2026-07-18): "1813. Ab.l 1. Mont" — April 1, 1813. The return reports strength "durante año de 1812" (a year-end return filed the following April), and follows a Dec 1812 entry on the same leaf.
note: First year-level error found by the audit. Class: year-digit misread, likely seduced by the content's own year.

was: Sargento "José Mª Mederos" (SB artillery detachment return).
reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #58/200, 2026-07-18): "Sarg.to José Mª MEDRANO" — corroborated by the same sergeant on C-A 18's leaf n403 (SF detachment, 1828).
note: Class: name misread.

was: Scan pin n440 / orig. p. 334+ for Figueroa's formal Tulares-expedition Instructions to Alf. Dámaso Antonio Rodríguez.
reads: n440–n441 carry the surrounding cluster (Villalpando extradition, Figueroa→LA alcalde, Carrillo→Figueroa, pp. 498–504) — the formal Instructions are not at the pin, and '334+' matches no page in the cluster.
note: Blind sample audit #59/200: the audit's second broken pin. The expedition and Rodríguez's command are corroborated at n441; the Instructions document needs re-location at a C-A 19 residual pass.

reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #72/200, 2026-07-18): "1785 - Feb.º 19 - Mont." — February 19, not 14.
note: Class: day-digit misread (9 read as 4) — a second digit pair alongside the audit's three 8-misreads. The 'toma del Pueblo… Vallejo el Director' quotation verified exact.

was: Author recorded as M. G. Vallejo (the Padrés sumaria letter).
reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #85/200, 2026-07-18): the header reads "Ygn.º del Valle á Figueroa" — IGNACIO DEL VALLE, and the content (the mission-reconnaissance and matanzas commissions, his attachment to the SF comandancia) confirms it.
note: Class: name misattribution — materially changes the actor; the People-register counts for Vallejo/del Valle are each off by one until re-ingest.

was: Quoted the adjacent notice as LA "libre de peligro de incursión."
reads: The leaf (full-resolution crop) reads "libre de peligro de invas.ⁿ" — invasión, "pr José Castro."
note: Blind sample audit #88/200. Class: misquoted word inside quotation marks — quotes must match the leaf exactly; the Stearns Junta-convening substance is otherwise accurate.

was: "Requests confirmation of his new post on the pueblo's behalf, pledging obedience."
reads: The leaf reads "Lo felicita á nombre del pueblo por su nuevo empleo protestándole obediencia" — the alcalde CONGRATULATES the (new) Governor on the GOVERNOR'S post, pledging obedience; nothing is requested.
note: Blind sample audit #89/200: the audit's first substance misconstrual (subject/object inverted). Date, persons, and pin all correct.

was: The May 6, 1843 item placed at "San José Misión"; second orig-page value 457.
reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #93/200, 2026-07-18): "1843 Mayo 6 Sta Ines (Mis.ⁿ)" — SANTA INÉS — and the page reads 437.
note: Classes: place misread + page-digit (3↔5). The record's other items (Den/Dos Pueblos, Camarillo, Bases Orgánicas) verified verbatim.

was: Second item rendered as the subject "wanders about with a passport"; principal guessed as "Pascual Batista[?]."
reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #97/200, 2026-07-18): "Que NAVARRO está con pasaporte" — a second named person, Navarro, holds the passport; and the principal reads "Pascual BOTILLER."
note: Classes: name-as-phrase misconstrual + flagged-name resolution (the [?] did its job on Botiller; Navarro was dropped entirely).

was: Proposed Juez de Paz propietario guessed as "Enrique Domínguez[?]."
reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #102/200, 2026-07-18): "á Enrique Domingo FITCH" — Henry Delano Fitch, the San Diego merchant.
note: A flagged uncertainty resolving to a significant identification — filed as a resolution, not an error; the record's [?] flag worked as designed.

reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #108/200, 2026-07-18): "1840 - Nov.ᵉ 30 - Mont." — November 30, not 20.
note: Class: day-digit misread (3 read as 2) — the audit's digit family now spans 8→2/3/6, 9→4, 3→2.

reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #110/200, 2026-07-18): the block header reads "1839. Mont." — and the content (Peña taking possession of the Prefectura, Francisco Castillo named its secretary) belongs to the 1839 prefecture rollout; prefectures did not operate in California in 1837.
note: Class: year-digit misread (9 read as 7 — a new pair in the digit family), with content-consistency corroboration.

reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #114/200, 2026-07-18): "1842, Junio 19, México" — June 19, not 14.
note: Class: day-digit misread, the second 9→4 in the audit (with ca23-d76) — a recurrent pair in Savage's hand.

was: Quoted Figueroa as agreeing to avoid burdens that would 'aniquilar la naciente y única industria del país.'
reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #120/200, 2026-07-19): the leaf — itself quoting the letter — reads "por cuya exacción se ARRUINARÍA la naciente y única industria del país."
note: Class: misquoted word inside quotation marks (second instance, with ca31-d7). The record's substance (alcabalas without oppressive burdens; afora by value; uniformize; consult Mexico) is otherwise exact.

reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #122/200, 2026-07-19): "1845 - Marzo 17 - Los Angeles" — March 17, not 1; the leaf's page sequence (237–38, 239, 240–41) runs chronologically Mar 1 → 12 → 17, corroborating.
note: Class: day-digit (17 read as 1 — a dropped second digit).

was: Quoted the militia bando as issued 'en virtud de la Superior orden de 11 de Mayo…'
reads: Full-resolution crop (blind sample audit #127/200, 2026-07-19): the leaf reads "en virtud de la Sup.ᵐᵃ [Suprema] orden de 11 de Mayo últ.º" — Suprema, not Superior.
note: Class: abbreviation mis-expanded inside quotation marks (third quote-class instance). Also: the document sits at p.276; the recorded 276–277 includes the following entry's page.

was: Recipient recorded as the Minister of War.
reads: The docket (blind sample audit #138/200, 2026-07-19) reads "Argüello al Mntro de ESTADO: Naufragio de un Buqe" — the letter goes to the Minister of State, forwarding the parte rendered to the Minister of War.
note: Class: recipient confusion (inner addressee promoted to recipient). The 'Naufragio de un Buque' docket, date, and pin verified exact.

was: 28 licencias "para individuos de la Compañía de S. Fernando."
reads: Full-resolution crops (blind sample audit #146/200, 2026-07-19): the body reads "pª indivs de Compª de SU MANDO" — of his command — and the addressee line reads "Comᵗᵉ de Compª de Cabª permanente de Mon[terrey]."
note: Class: phrase misread ('su mando' → 'S. Fernando'), inventing a unit identification. The 28-licencias substance and pins verified exact.
